mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months following a rate cut, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.5%, weak labor market conditions suppress consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in >60% of months where UNRATE exceeds 9.5%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and builder confidence, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns in >50% of co-occurring months, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals expanding manufacturing output and tightening capacity utilization, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during expansion phases.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months with rising INDPRO during expansions, or the signal reverses direction in late-cycle regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 220.0, accelerating inflation expectations trigger fears of monetary tightening and margin compression, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in >60% of months where CPIAUCSL crosses above 220.0, or the threshold effect vanishes in rolling window analysis.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and declines in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative 60-day returns in >50% of observed months, or the interaction effect is dominated by single-series noise in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects broad economic expansion and rising corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of quarters following positive GDPC1 growth, or the signal shows zero predictive power in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: When the index of industrial production falls below 98.0, severe manufacturing contraction signals impending recessionary pressures and earnings downgrades, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in >60% of months where INDPRO drops below 98.0, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions significantly.
